_comment: COMMUNITY (non-official) data sources, kept separate from official-links.json. GGG publishes NO official skill-gem, support-gem, or base-item export (only the passive/atlas trees). Per docs/research/14-development-implementation-plan.md, community-extracted game data is accepted ONLY to resolve build ids and power .build export, with clear attribution and no bulk-republishing as site content. The underlying game data is (c) Grinding Gear Games; the tooling licenses are noted per source.

sources.label: Path of Building Community (PoE2) — gem data
sources.license: Tooling MIT; underlying gem data (c) Grinding Gear Games.
sources.notes: Authoritative community source for the EXACT in-game .build gem metadata id, read from each gem's `gameId` field. Cross-validated against GGG's official .build example (SkillGemEarthquake, SkillGemBoneshatter, SupportGemAftershock, SupportGemFastForward, SupportGemImpactShockwave all match). PoE2 gem paths are irregular (e.g. Spark is .../Gems/SkillGemSpark while Fireball/Blink are .../Gem/SkillGem...), so `gameId` is used per-gem rather than any blanket convention. Used only to resolve gem ids for builds and .build export, never republished as a database page.

sources.label: RePoE fork (PoE2) — base item data
sources.license: RePoE tooling MIT (c) 2016 brather1ng; underlying item data (c) Grinding Gear Games.
sources.notes: Community-extracted base item metadata (id, item class, drop level, attribute requirements). The official .build inventory_slots use a slot id + free-text hints (no base-item id), so this dataset is NOT consumed by .build export; it backs on-page exact base requirements only. Normalized to a compact equippable-bases index; the 7.5 MB raw is fetched transiently (pinned), not vendored.
